//: Glass Atrium ~/.claude/settings.json PERMISSIONS template (DF-22 reference — NOT auto-applied).
DEFECT: an rm NEGATION placed inside permissions.allow — e.g. "!Bash(rm:*)" — is likely INERT.
Claude Code's permission layer denies via the permissions.deny array; an allow-list negation is
not the documented deny mechanism, so "Bash(*)" + "!Bash(rm:*)" in allow does NOT reliably block
rm at the permission layer (the block-dangerous-commands.sh hook is a SEPARATE, later gate).
FIX: declare rm rules in permissions.deny (below). deny is evaluated with precedence over allow.
APPLY: merge ONLY the "permissions" object into your existing ~/.claude/settings.json (never wipe
other keys). wire_hooks (lib/ga-config-hooks.sh) never touches permissions, so this stays manual.
LIVE-VERIFY (see the "live_verify" key): confirm rm is blocked at the PERMISSION layer, not merely
HOOKS: the "hooks" block below documents the edit-time syntax gate (validate-edit-syntax.sh), a
PreToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory that runs bash -n / node --check / py_compile by extension allowlist.
It is advisory-first (warns on a syntax error; set SYNTAX_GATE_BLOCK=1 to block after burn-in) and
exempts templates. wire_hooks (from the ga-env.sh EXPECTED_HOOK_BINDINGS SoT) wires it automatically
with an absolute command path; this block is the manual-reference form.
live_verify: 1. Apply the permissions block above to ~/.claude/settings.json, then start a fresh 'claude' session.
2. Request an rm command, e.g. Bash: rm /tmp/ga-df22-probe
3. EXPECT: the call is DENIED at the permission layer (permission prompt / auto-deny), BEFORE any
hook fires. A pass = rm cannot execute. Contrast with the pre-fix state, where '!Bash(rm:*)' sat
inside permissions.allow and rm was NOT permission-denied (only the hook, if present, caught it).
4. Positive control: Bash: ls /tmp — MUST still be permitted (deny is scoped to rm only).
